anchoring_evidence: BG 2.15 caps the sāṅkhya block A and prefigures the sthita-prajña thread that will be elaborated in 2.54-2.72. The engineering layer correctly anchors on both: the qualifier (trained equanimity) flows from the substrate-doctrine of 2.11-2.13 and the discipline of 2.14, and is the precondition for the seasoned-operator state of mind. Tags ['sattva-clarity', 'deep-work', 'long-term-thinking', 'operator-system-coupling'] span the sāṅkhya / sthita-prajña edge.
hard_edge_preservation: PASS — the chapter's hard edge ('the steady mind is a real state, not an aspiration') is preserved. The engineering layer is precise: 'It is not a personality trait. It is a trained capacity.' Falsifiability rules out the cold-detached misreading.
counter_anti_thesis_check: PASS — the verse does not detach engineers from users (care is verified explicitly), does not absent consequence-weight (the long-horizon work the verse names is high-consequence), does not flatten svadharma (the verse explicitly applies to specific kinds of work, not all work).
progressive_build: PASS — at position 15, the verse correctly caps the sāṅkhya block A by stating the qualifier the prior verses have built toward. The progression is: 2.11 (diagnostic), 2.12 (eternality), 2.13 (analogical proof), 2.14 (discipline), 2.15 (qualifier). This is the right pedagogical arc. The verse prefigures the sthita-prajña block (2.54-2.72) without yet operating in its full vocabulary, which is the right restraint.
